From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Internet service

Not to be confused with [AOL On](/wiki/AOL_On "AOL On").

AOL TV

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Company type | [Subsidiary](/wiki/Subsidiary "Subsidiary") of [America Online](/wiki/America_Online "America Online") |
| Industry | [Internet Protocol television](/wiki/Internet_Protocol_television "Internet Protocol television") |
| Founded | June 2000; 25 years ago (2000-06) |
| Defunct | 2002 |
| Fate | Discontinued |
| Headquarters | [New York, NY](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") , [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| Area served | nationwide in USA |
| Key people | [Anne Bentley](/w/index.php?title=Anne_Bentley&action=edit&redlink=1 "Anne Bentley (page does not exist)") (spokeswomen) [David Nagel](/wiki/David_Nagel "David Nagel") (board member) [Tom Nagel](/wiki/Tom_Nagel "Tom Nagel") (board member) [James Barksdale](/wiki/James_Barksdale "James Barksdale") (board member) [Larry Ellison](/wiki/Larry_Ellison "Larry Ellison") (board member) [Mitchell Kertzman](/wiki/Mitchell_Kertzman "Mitchell Kertzman") (early CEO and president) [Philip Vachon](/w/index.php?title=Philip_Vachon&action=edit&redlink=1 "Philip Vachon (page does not exist)") (later CEO) |
| Products | [IPTV](/wiki/IPTV "IPTV") |
| [Parent](/wiki/Parent_company "Parent company") | [AOL](/wiki/AOL "AOL") |
| Website | aoltv.com (Not active) |

**AOL TV** was the name of both a [thin client](/wiki/Thin_client "Thin client") which uses a [television](/wiki/Television "Television") for display (rather than a [monitor](/wiki/Computer_display "Computer display")), and the [online service](/wiki/Online_service "Online service") that supports it, both of which were launched in June 2000 to compete with [WebTV](/wiki/MSN_TV "MSN TV").

The product and service were developed by [America Online](/wiki/America_Online "America Online"). While most [thin clients](/wiki/Thin_client "Thin client") developed in the mid-1990s were positioned as diskless workstations for corporate [intranets](/wiki/Local_area_network "Local area network"), AOL TV was positioned as a consumer device for web access. Since the device was a dedicated web browser appliance, the cost of licensing a [proprietary](/wiki/Proprietary_Software "Proprietary Software") [operating system](/wiki/Operating_system "Operating system") could be avoided. The cost of licensing a [proprietary](/wiki/Proprietary_Software "Proprietary Software") operating system is substantial for inexpensive devices.

The set top box for AOL TV was developed by NCI/Liberate using a [thin client](/wiki/Thin_client "Thin client") and manufactured by [Philips](/wiki/Philips "Philips").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

AOL TV discontinued sales in November 2002, although the service remained available to existing subscribers.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The service is no longer supported by AOL and the documentation has been removed from their servers.
